metadata.title: Carlos HOUNSINOU · Integrated Systems Architect & M&E Specialist
metadata.description: Carlos Hounsinou, Project Engineering Officer at the OIF. Expert in monitoring, evaluation and platform architecture for international institutions, donors and organizations.
metadata.keywords: monitoring evaluation, project engineering, OIF, systems architect, M&E consultant, Carlos Hounsinou

hero.tag: Project Engineering Officer · International Organisation of La Francophonie

items: CIHR · Canadian Institutes of Health Research

about.p1: I have built my career in service of a simple conviction: organisations that steer by data deliver more impact, and those that master their systems deserve to be served by architects, not by vendors. At the intersection of applied statistics, institutional monitoring-evaluation, and software design, I have been supporting donors, institutions, and private actors for thirteen years. From territorial municipalities to multilateral organisations, from bilateral European programmes to consumer products, my signature remains constant: methodological rigour in the service of architectures that last.
about.p2Part1: From Benin to Paris, via Belgium, I now serve the
about.p2Part2: as Project Engineering Officer, after designing platforms for international donors and launching my own SaaS product. A path that taught me to design, specify and ship systems that last.

timeline.title: Architect of M&E Modernisation
timeline.org: Organisation Internationale de la Francophonie · Paris
timeline.description: Designing an institutional platform to digitalise the steering of an international programme portfolio. From specification to governance, I am structuring a system that will transform decision-making in the field.

timeline.title: Growth Catalyst · Data Strategy
timeline.description: Built the group's data-driven steering system. Designed estimation tools and financial KPIs (cost, margin, profitability). Market intelligence and expansion into new segments.

timeline.title: Systems Designer for EU Donors
timeline.org: IBF International Consulting · RePaSOC Programme · Belgium & Benin
timeline.description: Implementation of two complete web platforms for monitoring and evaluation under the RePaSOC programme, funded by the European Union (consortium IBF International Consulting / NIRAS / QUAREIN). Refinement of the global logical framework, design and online deployment of the computerised system, training of operators (CPSC, Régie, MdSC, GIZ) and Technical Assistants.

timeline.title: Architect of Programming Tools
timeline.description: International Volunteer of La Francophonie (VIF) at the Coordination Service for Off-Site Units. Design and deployment of indicator monitoring tools for the 2019-2022 four-year programming. Support to national structures (RESIFS) on the complete strategic planning cycle.

timeline.title: Statistician · Health Research
timeline.description: Statistician within the Project Coordination of CHU de Québec in Benin, funded by the Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR) and the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation. Design of databases for public health research and improvement of living conditions of vulnerable populations ('Health Equity' project).

timeline.title: Builder of Territorial M&E Systems
timeline.description: Built the monitoring system for the Decennial Education Plan. Designed Access tools for the Municipal Development Plan (PDC) monitoring and financial management.

flagship.intro: A selection of seven flagship architectures, from institutional multilateral steering to my own SaaS product, including European donors, territorial governance and private operations. Each one demonstrates expertise in design, from strategy through to operational deployment.

testimonial.quote: Carlos transformed our monitoring & evaluation system. His methodological rigor combined with his mastery of digital tools enabled us to gain clarity and responsiveness in steering our projects.

items.metaDescription: Design of a secure monitoring & evaluation platform for a multilateral francophone organisation: modular architecture, GDPR compliance, multi-role access.
items.title: Programme portfolio steering platform
items.subtitle: International organisation · Paris
items.context: A multilateral francophone organisation managing a portfolio of international multi-stakeholder programmes needed to modernise its monitoring and evaluation setup. Processes relied on disparate office tools, making data consolidation and reporting to governance bodies laborious and unreliable.
items.challenge: Designing a secure institutional web platform that meets the strict confidentiality requirements of the multilateral sector, while offering a smooth enough experience to drive adoption among non-technical teams spread across headquarters and field representations.
approach: Detailed functional scoping with programme teams to model the portfolio and validation workflows.
Multi-role modular architecture with Row-Level Security ensuring isolation of sensitive data.
Adoption-oriented UX design: minimal interfaces, contextual in-app guides, user training.
Data governance: indicator registry, programme lifecycle management, complete audit trail.
items.results: First version validated by governance bodies, integration under way. The framework covers the full steering cycle, from programme planning to progress report generation.
items.stackDetail: Secure web architecture · Relational databases · GDPR compliance · Multi-role management · Row-Level Security

items.metaTitle: Project incubation and evaluation platform · Carlos Hounsinou
items.metaDescription: Full-stack platform for the complete development project lifecycle: RBM training, SMART indicators, encapsulated AI, automated reporting.
items.title: Project and programme incubation and steering platform
items.subtitle: Institutional organisation · Paris
items.context: An institution supporting development project owners needed a unified framework to cover the full lifecycle: training in Results-Based Management standards, guided incubation, SMART indicator tracking, performance measurement, mixed analysis and report production.
items.challenge: Integrating into a single platform capabilities ranging from pedagogy to advanced analytics, with encapsulated generative AI preventing hallucinations on institutional documents, and a secure architecture supporting distinct access profiles for project owners, coaches and administrators.
approach: 8-module coupled architecture covering training, incubation, SMART indicators, measurement, analysis and reporting.
50+ API endpoints with complex state management, notifications and scheduled alerts.
Encapsulated AI: RAG on proprietary document base, Vision module, usage quotas per profile, anti-hallucination guardrails.
Security: multi-role RLS, complete audit log, sensitive data encryption.
Collaborative workflow: project assignment, review cycles, institutional validation.
items.results: Platform delivered and deployed in production. Adopted by the institution for its next project cohort. Analytics and reporting modules significantly reduced the time needed to produce progress reports.
items.stackDetail: Full-stack architecture · Vector base · Encapsulated generative AI (RAG + Vision) · Multi-role workflow · RLS · Scheduled alerts · Automated reporting

items.metaTitle: Programme portfolio valorisation platform · Carlos Hounsinou
items.metaDescription: Institutional platform combining data storytelling, cartography and AI assistant to valorise a portfolio of strategic projects: RLS, anti-hallucination.
items.title: Programme portfolio valorisation platform
items.subtitle: Institutional organisation · Paris
items.context: An international institution wanted to make its added value visible through its portfolio of strategic projects. The data existed but was buried in PDF reports inaccessible to the general public and potential partners.
items.challenge: Transforming static reports into interactive web experiences while guaranteeing the accuracy of information provided by the conversational assistant, a major institutional reputation issue, and managing the editorial workflow for teams with heterogeneous digital skills.
approach: Multi-role editorial CMS enabling teams to create, review and publish structured project pages.
Data storytelling: data visualisations, key indicators, impact timeline per project.
Analytical dashboard offering a portfolio overview: geographic spread, themes, progress levels.
Interactive map exploration of projects by geographic zone.
Conversational assistant powered by a proprietary vector base, with anti-hallucination guardrails and response audit.
items.results: Pilot deployed in production. Adopted by the editorial team from day one. The conversational assistant responds to external partner questions with precision benchmarked against the institutional reference base.
items.stackDetail: Full-stack architecture · Proprietary vector base · Encapsulated AI · Web cartography · Multi-role editorial workflow · RLS · Anti-hallucination

items.metaTitle: M&E platforms · EU-funded projects · Carlos Hounsinou
items.metaDescription: Design and deployment of two web monitoring & evaluation platforms for EU-funded programmes: OECD-DAC standards, multi-stakeholder, operator training.
items.title: Monitoring platforms · EU-funded projects
items.subtitle: IBF International Consulting · Belgium & Benin
items.context: As part of a consulting mission for IBF International Consulting, I was responsible for designing and deploying the monitoring and evaluation tools for a European Union-funded development programme involving partners in Belgium and West Africa.
items.challenge: Designing two distinct platforms each tailored to the specific needs of their users, coordinators, donors and implementing partners, while ensuring interoperability and compliance with OECD-DAC donor reporting standards.
approach: Methodological framing: refinement of the overall logical framework, definition of indicators and targets.
Design of two dedicated web platforms adapted to the skill levels of field users.
OECD-DAC aligned architecture for multi-donor reporting: indicators, outputs, outcomes and impact.
Training of operators (CPSC, Régie, MdSC) and Technical Assistants in person and remotely.
User documentation and change management support.
items.results: Both platforms were deployed and adopted across all programme stakeholders. Operator training enabled quick uptake and autonomous use from the first months.
items.stackDetail: Web architecture · OECD-DAC standards · Multi-donor reporting · Multi-role access · Operator training

items.metaDescription: Secure platform for data-driven local development steering: annual campaigns, participatory certification, audit trail. Geographic scope: West Africa.
items.title: Indicator-based steering platform
items.subtitle: Territorial governance · West Africa
items.context: A territorial governance body in West Africa wanted to move from managing its local development plan with fragmented Excel files to a shared web platform guaranteeing the quality and traceability of data produced by multiple sectoral actors.
items.challenge: Designing a participatory certification workflow adapted to sectoral focal points with limited digital literacy, while ensuring the rigour of published data: structured collection, automated quality controls and a tamper-proof audit trail.
approach: 3 distinct access profiles (Admin / Editor / Reader) with RLS ensuring data isolation.
Structured annual campaigns: collection windows, automated reminders, close-out and archiving.
Automated quality controls on entry: value ranges, cross-indicator consistency checks, duplicate detection.
Participatory certification: sequential validation by sectoral focal points before publication.
Complete audit log: full traceability of every change with timestamp and author identification.
Responsive interface adapted to field connectivity constraints.
items.results: First version tested with sectoral focal points. Feedback enabled refinement of the certification workflow and the data entry interface. Full deployment under way.
items.stackDetail: Secure web architecture · Multi-role RLS · Indicator registry · Participatory certification workflow · Audit trail · Responsive interface

items.metaTitle: Cap Citoyen · Civic exam application · Carlos Hounsinou
items.metaDescription: EdTech SaaS for preparing the French civic naturalisation exam: 800+ questions, AI coach, audio flash-cards, available on Web and Android.
items.title: Cap Citoyen · French civic exam
items.subtitle: cap-citoyen.fr · France
items.context: The mandatory civic exam for naturalisation applications, effective from January 1, 2026, created a market of 100 000 candidates per year. At Cap Citoyen's launch, no quality application addressed this emerging need with a rigorous pedagogical approach.
items.challenge: Designing an engaging learning experience for an extremely heterogeneous audience (ages, backgrounds, digital skill levels), combining diverse formats (quiz, audio, flash-cards, exam simulation) with an AI coach capable of adapting to each user's level.
approach: Next.js PWA architecture delivering a native mobile experience without native-app constraints.
800+ questions structured by theme, with progressive difficulty and pedagogical explanations.
100+ audio tracks (ElevenLabs) for candidates with reading difficulties.
Flash-card mode for memorisation and exam simulation with official timer.
Integrated AI coach combining Anthropic API and GPT-4o-mini, adapted to each user's level and gaps.
Monetisation via Stripe: monthly subscription and lifetime access. Android (Google Play) and PWA deployment.
iOS under Apple review.
items.results: Live on Web and Android (Google Play). Growing adoption since launch, with high satisfaction measured through Google Play reviews. iOS in final validation.
items.stackDetail: Next.js · Anthropic API · GPT-4o-mini · ElevenLabs · Stripe · PWA · Android · PostgreSQL · Vector search

items.metaTitle: Field operations management platform · Carlos Hounsinou
items.metaDescription: Design and deployment of a no-code business platform for field operations: agent portfolio, geolocation, scheduling, quotes and invoicing.
items.title: Integrated scheduling and mission management platform
items.subtitle: Private actor · Operational sector
items.context: A private operational sector company managed a fleet of field agents with complex schedules, geolocated missions and an entirely manual billing chain. Information scattered across Excel spreadsheets, Word files and informal exchanges was generating costly errors and excessive time-to-invoice.
items.challenge: Integrating into a single coherent experience the agent portfolio, site geolocation, automated mission planning, calendar tracking, quote generation and invoicing, within a tight delivery timeline imposed by the operational context.
approach: Accelerated no-code architecture to reduce time-to-market while guaranteeing robustness.
Agent portfolio: profiles, skills, availability, geolocated intervention zones.
Automated scheduling: agent assignment to missions based on proximity and availability criteria.
Calendar tracking: weekly and monthly views, scheduling conflict alerts.
Automated quote generation and invoicing: configurable templates, email delivery, payment tracking.
Responsive mobile app for field agents: mission consultation, digital signatures.
API interoperability with the client's existing tools.
items.results: Platform deployed in production and adopted by field teams from the first week. Time-to-invoice reduced by over 60%. Assignment errors virtually eliminated through automated availability checks.
items.stackDetail: No-code platform · Third-party APIs · Web cartography · Automated workflow · Mobile app · Document generation
